raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 519 would provide the first updates to honey labeling in California since 1978.",
    "Honey country of origin labels would need to match the same font and size of the USDA Grade and be placed on the same panel as the grade statement.",
    "Honey laundering occurs when honey is sold as pure but is mixed with other fillers or sweeteners, is of false origin, or is of poor quality.",
    "In June 2020, Sweetwater Labs found that 35-40% of the consumer-instigated honey tested was laundered.",
    "Current state and federal provisions require labels to contain source and blend information since honey produced globally can have higher exposure to toxins, antibiotics, allergens, and environmental pollutants.",
    "Ambiguity in the laws has created discrepancies, resulting in cryptic and obscure labels.",
    "California is one of the top producers in the country, producing over 3.2 million pounds of honey every year.",
    "Ventura County produces roughly 20% of the honey in California."
  ]
}
```
